essence: Uses AI, optimisation algorithms, and real‑time analytics to design, refine, and automate manufacturing processes end‑to‑end.

identity.title: AI Process Engineer
identity.summary: Improves manufacturing workflows using AI‑driven optimisation, predictive modelling, and adaptive control, ensuring processes are efficient, stable, and capable of autonomous adjustment.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £145k-£190k+.

qualifications: Experience in process engineering, automation, or AI‑driven optimisation
Knowledge of CNC, robotics, and digital manufacturing workflows
Skills in machine learning, statistical modelling, and process simulation
Understanding of IIoT, MES, SCADA, and real‑time control systems

lifestyle.description: AI process engineers work in aerospace, robotics, automotive, and Industry 4.0 environments. They design and optimise manufacturing processes using AI‑driven insights, enabling adaptive, efficient, and increasingly autonomous workflows. The role suits individuals who enjoy optimisation, systems thinking, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Optimises manufacturing processes using AI and analytics
Implements adaptive control strategies for real‑time adjustment
Builds predictive models for process stability and efficiency
Collaborates with production, AI, and automation teams
Ensures processes are scalable, reliable, and ready for autonomy
